name: PortaPack Mayhem (HackRF One / Pro / PortaRF)
label: PortaPack Mayhem — HackRF SDR firmware
description: portapack-mayhem/mayhem-firmware — the flagship HackRF+PortaPack SDR firmware (RX-heavy: ADS-B, POCSAG/ACARS/APRS, TPMS, spectrum recon; and TX apps on protected/emergency bands). Transmitting on those bands unlicensed is illegal -> illegal-tx label. CC only flashes the firmware; Mayhem's own on-device apps are the transmitters. CC authors no TX payload.

support_files.dfu_unbrick: firmware/hackrf_usb.dfu (1fc9:000c, via CC's existing dfu backend)

note: App is written by hackrf_spiflash -R -w (libusb vendor cmd), NOT dfu-util (dfu only RAM-loads a USB stack for unbrick) -> new hackrf_spiflash backend. The flashable .bin is a ZIP MEMBER inside FIRMWARE_mayhem_vX.Y.Z.zip -> resolver needs zip-extract + variant-member pick (hackrf 1MB / portarf 2MB / hpro 4MB). Whole-flash raw image from 0x0 (no app_offset math). HackRF Pro (hpro/PRALINE) is WIP/buggy in v2.4.0 -> experimental. danger illegal-tx (bundles EPIRB/SAME/P25/POCSAG TX apps) but genuinely dual-use with a large legit RX side; label-and-warn, never block; CC authors NO TX. Optional DFU-unbrick sub-flow via CC's existing dfu backend (1fc9:000c hackrf_usb.dfu) + factory-restore via hackrf_usb.bin. SHA read from the zip but kept verify: until a real HackRF One flash. Opens CC's SDR layer.
